mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains near zero, signaling continued monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates and increases equity valuations. Sustained low rates drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors remain in risk-on positioning.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential construction demand and household formation, which cascades into increased consumer spending on durables and services. This demand acceleration drives corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: housing recovery phase with accelerating construction
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 58% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month increases, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength. Sustained production increases above trend indicate robust economic activity that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: expansion phase with accelerating industrial activity
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates or turns negative, signaling weakening labor demand and potential economic slowdown. This deterioration triggers equity risk-off positioning and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession warning
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 0.1%, or signal produces false positives in more than 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation measured by CPI combined with elevated unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that erodes real returns and increases policy uncertainty. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors demand higher risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-month growth exceeds 2.5% AND UNRATE remains above 6.0%
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with conflicting policy signals
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 52% of instances where both conditions are met, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening aggregate demand and economic momentum loss. This slowdown produces negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate falls below 0.5% annualized
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in less than 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 0.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates while housing starts remain elevated, indicating synchronized strength in consumer and residential demand. This dual demand expansion drives positive earnings revisions and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4% AND HOUST month-over-month growth exceeds 3%
regime_hint: synchronized demand acceleration in consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 60% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST conditions are met, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate specification.
